description: Phase 3 of ADR-2605021400. Two adapters that bind IExperimentPort (from wp-experiment-loop-p2) to its real backends. (1) SpacetimeExperimentAdapter — primary persistence against the objective/hypothesis/verdict tables in hexflo-coordination, calling reducers via the SpacetimeDB Rust client. (2) StashExperimentAdapter — decorator that wraps an inner IExperimentPort plus an IConsolidationMemoryPort (from ADR-2604261430) and projection-mirrors hypothesis_create + hypothesis_update_status + objective_create into stash so the consolidation pipeline can reason about them. NOTE: end-to-end verification requires a live SpacetimeDB instance; the cargo check gate is sufficient when no instance is reachable, but P3.4 integration test is skipped unless SPACETIMEDB_HOST is set.

phases.description: Adapter wiring. Tier 1 (secondary adapters per ADR-001 rule 4). Gate: cargo check -p hex-nexus. Integration test gated on SPACETIMEDB_HOST so it skips in environments without a running SpacetimeDB. Composition-root wiring is part of P3.3 — only composition-root.* may import from adapters (ADR-001 rule 6).
phases.name: SpacetimeExperimentAdapter + StashExperimentAdapter + composition wiring

tasks.description: Create hex-nexus/src/adapters/spacetime_experiment.rs implementing `IExperimentPort` against the experiment tables in hexflo-coordination (objective, hypothesis, verdict — added in wp-experiment-loop-p2). Pattern matches existing adapters in this directory: pub struct SpacetimeExperimentAdapter { client: Arc<SpacetimeClient> }; #[async_trait] impl IExperimentPort for SpacetimeExperimentAdapter. For each port method: (a) translate hex_core::domain::experiment domain types into reducer args (enum variants → string columns, e.g. ObjectivePriority::High → "high", ComparisonOperator::WithinRange{tolerance} → comparison="within_range" + comparison_tolerance=tolerance, all other variants set comparison_tolerance=0.0); (b) call the matching reducer via the SpacetimeDB client; (c) map errors to ExperimentError (Backend(String) for transport failures, ObjectiveNotFound/HypothesisNotFound/VerdictAlreadyArchived for known reducer error patterns). For `_get` and `_list` methods, query the table directly via the SpacetimeDB row-subscription API; convert row → domain type by parsing the string-encoded enum back to its variant (return ExperimentError::Backend("invalid status: ...") on unknown strings). Keep each conversion fn small and unit-testable: e.g. `fn objective_row_to_domain(row: ObjectiveRow) -> Result<Objective, ExperimentError>`.
tasks.name: Add SpacetimeExperimentAdapter implementing IExperimentPort

tasks.description: Create hex-nexus/src/adapters/stash_experiment.rs containing `StashExperimentAdapter<Inner>` which is a decorator over an inner `IExperimentPort` impl plus an `IConsolidationMemoryPort` impl. Fields: `inner: Inner`, `stash: Arc<dyn IConsolidationMemoryPort>`, `enabled: bool` (read from config; when false, projection is a no-op and only `inner` is touched — same blast-radius escape valve as the NoopConsolidationAdapter in ADR-2604261430). For each write method (objective_create, hypothesis_create, hypothesis_update_status, verdict_record), call inner first; on success, also project to stash: hypothesis_create → stash.create_hypothesis(content, verification_plan); objective_create → stash.create_goal(name, parent_id, priority); verdict_record with decision=Rollback → stash.create_failure(content, reason, lesson, goal_id=objective_id). Read methods (objective_get/_list, hypothesis_get/_list_for_objective, verdict_get/_list_for_objective) are pure delegation to inner — stash is not the source of truth for queries. Projection failures are LOGGED, not surfaced — the inner write has already succeeded; failing the call would lose data. The projection is best-effort.
tasks.name: Add StashExperimentAdapter projection decorator

tasks.description: Wire the experiment adapter into the hex-nexus composition root. Find where SpacetimeStateAdapter is constructed (grep for `SpacetimeStateAdapter::new` or similar) — likely in hex-nexus/src/main.rs or hex-nexus/src/composition/. Inject `SpacetimeExperimentAdapter` alongside it; if `consolidation.enabled` is true in .hex/project.json AND a stash sidecar is reachable, wrap it with `StashExperimentAdapter::new(inner, stash, enabled=true)` — otherwise pass `inner` directly. Expose the resulting `Arc<dyn IExperimentPort>` on the AgentManager / composition struct so REST routes (P4 of the ADR) can pull it. Per ADR-001 rule 6, only the composition-root file may import from adapters/ — verify with `grep -rn 'use crate::adapters::spacetime_experiment\|use crate::adapters::stash_experiment' hex-nexus/src/` and ensure only the composition file matches.
tasks.name: Wire experiment adapter into composition root

tasks.description: Add an integration test at hex-nexus/tests/spacetime_experiment_integration.rs that exercises the full create/get/list/update_status/record cycle against a real SpacetimeDB instance. Test is gated by env: `if std::env::var("SPACETIMEDB_HOST").is_err() { eprintln!("skipping: SPACETIMEDB_HOST not set"); return; }`. Steps: (1) construct adapter against the env-pointed instance; (2) objective_create with a fresh UUID; (3) objective_get returns Some with the same fields; (4) hypothesis_create against that objective_id — expects success; (5) hypothesis_create against a bogus objective_id — expects ExperimentError::ObjectiveNotFound (or Backend with message containing "not found"); (6) verdict_record with decision=Graduate; (7) verdict_list_for_objective returns the recorded verdict; (8) cleanup: hypothesis_update_status → "abandoned" not valid, verify it returns Err. Assert all error paths return ExperimentError::Backend with descriptive messages, never panicking.
tasks.name: End-to-end integration test against live SpacetimeDB (env-gated)
